Description: Configure SQLx connection pool with appropriate limits and timeouts for production workloads
Do: Set max_connections based on workload; configure acquire_timeout and idle_timeout; use .close().await for graceful shutdown
Dont: Create new connections per query; use default pool settings without tuning
Code Bad: let pool = PgPool::connect(&database_url).await?;\n// default: 10 connections, no idle timeout, no acquire timeout\n// may exhaust connections or leak idle ones

Guideline: Use compile-time verified queries with sqlx::query!
Description: sqlx::query!() macro verifies SQL against the database at compile time catching typos and type mismatches
Do: Use query!() and query_as!() for compile-time verification; use DATABASE_URL env var; run cargo sqlx prepare for offline mode
Dont: Use string-based queries without verification; concatenate user input into SQL

Description: Manage schema changes with versioned migration files and the sqlx migrate command
Do: Use sqlx migrate add; run migrations at startup or in CI; keep migrations reversible; test migrations
Dont: Modify database schema manually; skip migration testing; use raw DDL in application code

Description: Wrap multiple queries in a transaction to ensure all-or-nothing semantics
Do: Use pool.begin() for transactions; commit explicitly; rollback on any error via Drop; use FOR UPDATE for row locking
Dont: Execute related queries without transactions; assume partial success is acceptable

Guideline: Use SeaORM or Diesel for complex domain models
Description: For complex CRUD with many relations use an ORM; SQLx for simpler direct SQL needs
Do: Choose SQLx for simple queries and full SQL control; SeaORM for async ORM with migration tools; Diesel for compile-time safe sync ORM
Dont: Mix ORMs in the same project; use ORM for every query including complex analytics

Description: Define repository traits to decouple business logic from database implementation
Do: Define async trait Repository with CRUD methods; implement for SQLx/Diesel; inject via generics or trait objects; mock in tests
Dont: Call SQLx queries directly in handlers; spread database access across the codebase

Guideline: Use deadpool-redis for async Redis access
Description: Configure Redis connection pooling with deadpool-redis for caching and session management
Do: Use deadpool-redis for async pooling; set appropriate pool size; use pipeline for batching; handle connection errors gracefully
Dont: Create new Redis connections per request; use blocking redis client in async code
